home_injuries_players: Santiago Giménez — ankle injury (surgery earlier in 2026); returned to full training and cleared to feature, though fitness was monitored in the lead-up
away_injuries_players: Aubrey Modiba — hamstring injury suffered in CAF Champions League final; completed first full training session, declared available by coach Hugo Broos
matches.home_morale: high — Mexico are unbeaten in their last 8 matches (W6, D2) heading into the tournament, including a 5-1 demolition of Serbia in their final warm-up, and carry enormous home-crowd energy at a sold-out Estadio Azteca.
matches.away_morale: medium — South Africa secured a historic return to the World Cup by topping their CAF qualifying group, but recent form has been poor (0 wins in last 4 matches) and they enter as heavy underdogs against a host nation.
key_news: Mexico vs. South Africa opens the 2026 FIFA World Cup at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City — a rematch of the iconic 2010 World Cup opener that ended 1-1, with Estadio Azteca becoming the first stadium to host three World Cup opening matches (1970, 1986, 2026).

Santiago Giménez cleared to play after ankle surgery; Aubrey Modiba declared fit by South Africa coach Hugo Broos after completing full training despite hamstring scare — both squads near full strength for the tournament opener.

Mexico coach Javier Aguirre warns El Tri will 'not take anything for granted' against Bafana Bafana, while an opening ceremony headlined by Shakira, J Balvin, and Tyla will precede kickoff before 80,000+ fans.
17-year-old Gilberto Mora tipped as a potential World Cup breakout star for Mexico, as El Tri look to go beyond the round-of-16 for the first time in their history.

home_injuries_players: Kim Tae-hyeon — left ankle injury, ruled out of group stage
Cho Yu-min — foot injury, replaced in squad by Cho Wi-je
Bae Jun-ho — ankle injury, major doubt for match
away_injuries_players: Vaclav Cerny — injury, confirmed miss for entire tournament
matches.home_morale: medium — Korea recovered with back-to-back friendly wins over El Salvador (1-0) and Trinidad & Tobago (5-0) ahead of the tournament, but earlier losses to Austria and Ivory Coast by an aggregate 5-0 margin still loom large.
matches.away_morale: high — Czechia arrive on a five-match winning run, including a 3-1 warm-up win over Guatemala and dramatic back-to-back penalty shootout wins over Ireland and Denmark to qualify, demonstrating strong mental resilience.
key_news: Korea Republic vs Czechia kicks off World Cup 2026 Group A action at Estadio Akron in Guadalajara on June 11 at 10:00 PM ET — the first-ever competitive meeting between the two nations.

Czechia return to the FIFA World Cup for the first time in 20 years after dramatic penalty shootout wins over Ireland and Denmark in the UEFA play-offs, while South Korea face questions over defensive cohesion after a 5-0 aggregate loss to Ivory Coast and Austria in March friendlies.

Bae Jun-ho is a major doubt for Korea with an ankle injury, while Czechia are without creative winger Vaclav Cerny (injury) for the entire tournament — Lyon's Pavel Sulc is expected to step up in his absence.

Czech coach Miroslav Koubek praised Son Heung-min as 'a Korean legend' and warned of South Korea's attacking threat ahead of the Group A opener, with Patrik Schick set as Czechia's focal point up front.
